message:
fix(wasm): prevent premature GC of IndexedDB callback closures

Replace Closure::once_into_js with Closure::new for all IDB request,
open-request, and transaction event handlers. The once_into_js variant
transfers ownership to JS and relies on the GC to prevent the closure
from being collected — but the GC can collect it before the callback
fires if no strong JS reference exists, leading to a use-after-free
panic ("closure invoked after being dropped").

The fix stores closures in an Rc<RefCell<Option<...>>> shared between
all callbacks on the same request, ensuring:
1. The closures are kept alive as long as the Promise constructor holds
   them (via the Rc reference in the closure captures).
2. When any callback fires (success, error, or blocked/abort), it takes
   ownership of the tuple via .take(), dropping all sibling closures
   deterministically instead of leaking them until GC.
3. The handler registration now uses .as_ref().unchecked_ref() on the
   owned Closure rather than calling .unchecked_ref() on a temporarily-
   owned JsValue, matching the wasm-bindgen ownership model.

Affected functions: await_request, await_open_request, await_transaction.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
